title: DCP Token-Savings Analysis (Last 100 Sessions)
summary: Quantify Dynamic Context Pruning (DCP) token + USD savings across the last 100 OpenCode sessions via a single self-contained HTML report, with overall totals, per-model breakdown (tokens saved + DCP call counts), and a total-savings headline. Reads authoritative DCP state files and opencode storage; Python 3 stdlib only; offline.
risks: Model attribution ambiguity (sessions span >1 model) -> per-block then majority fallback
Schema drift / missing fields crash mid-run -> try/except skip + --verify surfacing
Non-deterministic output (sort tie-breaks) -> stable sort keys + reproducibility check
validationCriteria: HTML contains all 3 sections; totals internally consistent
Reproducible on re-run; no destructive writes to storage
keyOutcomes: HTML report at artifacts/dcp-savings-report.html (25,683 bytes, self-contained, compound savings analysis)
100 sessions analyzed (empty sessions excluded): 194.9M tokens used, 2.09M one-time saved (1.07%), 64.6M compound saved (33.15%) with 30.9x multiplier
11 models tracked; top saver: zai-coding-plan/glm-5.2 (22.8M compound tokens, 35.31%); 6 models with DCP calls, 5 with zero
Compound effect: DCP compresses context once, savings cascade across ~40 subsequent requests per session
Data source: SQLite opencode.db (sessions/messages/parts) + DCP JSON files (savings); empty sessions filtered via SQL
All 8 acceptance criteria PASS (A1-A8); --verify exit code 0 (7 checks including multiplier validation)
